posts.caption:
3 eye myths your optometrist wishes you'd stop believing:

1. Reading in the dark damages your eyes — it doesn't
2. Carrots fix your vision — Vitamin A helps, but won't reverse damage
3. Eye exercises don't work — clinical studies say they DO

The science is clear. Train your eyes.

#EyeMyths #EyeHealth #Axeo #VisionCare #DigitalEyeStrain

Your browser can now test your vision. No app needed.

Snellen acuity test + Ishihara color vision — both free at axeo.vision/test

Results in 2 minutes.

#VisionTest #EyeExam #FreeTest #Axeo #EyeHealth

Follow along: 60-second eye workout you can do right now.

1. Palming — warm hands, cover closed eyes (20s)
2. Focus Shift — look near, then far (20s)
3. Blink rapidly 10 times (20s)

Your eyes just got a mini-vacation.

#EyeWorkout #EyeExercises #Axeo #DigitalDetox #ScreenBreak

True or false: Blue light glasses prevent eye damage.

FALSE. Studies show blue light from screens doesn't cause permanent damage.

What DOES help: the 20-20-20 rule. Every 20 min, look 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Axeo has a built-in 20-20-20 reminder.

#BlueLightGlasses #EyeFacts #Axeo #ScreenTime #EyeCare

Sunday reset: 3-minute eye relaxation routine.

1. Close eyes, breathe deeply (30s)
2. Palming — warm hands over eyes (60s)
3. Slow figure-8 eye movement (30s)
4. Look out the window at the farthest point (30s)
5. Blink softly 20 times (30s)

You just gave your eyes a spa day.

#SundayReset #SelfCare #EyeCare #Axeo #Wellness
